provenance.source_corpus: Interactive effects of vegetation and water table depth on belowground C and N mobilization and greenhouse gas emissions in a restored peatland

source_spans.text: Maintaining a high water table is key to reducing peatland C losses.
dry (low water table) area were identified
presence of cottongrass
Dry plots had significantly higher concentrations of DOC in porewater
Emissions of CH4 were significantly higher in the presence of cottongrass
The presence of cottongrass tussocks clearly contributed to increased C sequestration
ecosystem respiration (ER) was higher in wet plots as compared to dry plots
Microbial biomass C in peat samples showed larger concentrations in the top layer... strongest in wet plots
concentration of total dissolved nitrogen (TDN) in porewater was significantly larger in wet plots with the presence of cottongrass plants
DOC concentration in porewater were higher at the dry sites... suggesting greater peat degradation
